sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Nantes have struggled in their last 5 matches, managing only 3 draws and 2 losses with no wins, scoring just 3 goals. Montpellier are in even worse form, with a 14-match winless streak, including 4 losses and 1 draw in their last 5, conceding heavily (12 goals). This suggests both teams are low on confidence, but Montpellier's defense appears more vulnerable.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last 2 head-to-head matches, Nantes have the upper hand, winning 3-1 in the most recent encounter and drawing 1-1 prior to that. Nantes have not lost in their last 3 games against Montpellier, indicating a psychological edge for the home side.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Nantes, positioned 15th with 33 points, have scored 36 goals but conceded 52, showing defensive frailties. Montpellier, languishing at 18th with only 16 points, have a dismal record with just 23 goals scored and 76 conceded, the worst in the league. Nantes' slightly better attacking output and home advantage could be decisive.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Tactics
sections.text: Nantes' forward Moses Simon (7 goals, 9 assists) could exploit Montpellier's leaky defense, while Montpellier's Teji Savanier (2 goals, 5 assists) remains a creative threat despite their struggles. Nantes are likely to dominate possession at home, averaging 4.12 corners per game, while Montpellier's defensive setup may lead to fewer scoring chances.
